proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the binary features, feature_013 is associated with lower objective_response (response rate when feature_013=1 is lower than when feature_013=0).
Among the binary features, feature_008 is associated with higher objective_response (response rate when feature_008=1 is higher than when feature_008=0).
Among the binary features, feature_021 is associated with lower objective_response.
Among the binary features, feature_015 is associated with lower objective_response.
Among the binary features, feature_027 is associated with lower objective_response.
Among the binary features, feature_006, feature_005, feature_023, feature_011, feature_017, feature_019, and feature_004 are NOT associated with objective_response on the marginal scale.

analyses.code: chi2 of feature_013 vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: feature_013=1 has response rate 0.1535 vs feature_013=0 rate 0.3460 (n=27481 vs 22519); chi-square p ~ 0.

analyses.code: chi2 of feature_008 vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: feature_008=1 has response rate 0.3614 vs feature_008=0 rate 0.1589 (n=20076 vs 29924); chi-square p ~ 0.

analyses.code: chi2 of feature_021 vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: feature_021=1 has response rate 0.1497 vs feature_021=0 rate 0.2503; chi-square p = 5.43e-56.

analyses.code: chi2 of feature_015 vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: feature_015=1 has response rate 0.1599 vs feature_015=0 rate 0.2604; chi-square p = 1.90e-98.

analyses.code: chi2 of feature_027 vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: feature_027=1 has response rate 0.1760 vs feature_027=0 rate 0.2422; chi-square p = 2.97e-09.

analyses.code: chi2 for each of f006, f005, f023, f011, f017, f019, f004 vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: All seven features show response rate differences <0.005 between levels and chi-square p > 0.30 (no marginal association with objective_response).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (3-level ordinal: 0/1/2) shows monotonic decrease in objective_response with increasing level (i.e., negative association).
feature_010 (5-level ordinal: 6-10) is NOT associated with objective_response.
Higher feature_022 is associated with lower objective_response (continuous, marginal mean comparison).
Higher feature_020 is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher feature_024 is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher feature_002 is associated with higher objective_response.

feature_016 (age-like, range 30-90), feature_018, feature_009, feature_026, feature_028, feature_007, feature_014, feature_032, feature_031, feature_025, feature_003, feature_012, feature_029 show no detectable marginal association with objective_response.
feature_030 is constant (single value) and contributes no information.

analyses.code: groupby feature_001 then mean(objective_response); chi2
groupby feature_010 then mean(objective_response); chi2
Welch t-test of feature_022 between responders and non-responders
analyses.result_summary: feature_022 mean: responders 28.24 vs non-responders 47.23 (diff -18.99); Welch t = -25.08, p = 1.5e-137.

analyses.code: Welch t-test of feature_020 by objective_response
analyses.result_summary: feature_020 mean: responders 3.50 vs non-responders 3.95 (diff -0.45); Welch t = -11.58, p = 6.8e-31.

analyses.code: Welch t-test of feature_024 by objective_response
analyses.result_summary: feature_024 mean: responders 5.69 vs non-responders 6.23 (diff -0.53); Welch t = -5.75, p = 9.3e-09.

analyses.code: Welch t-test of feature_002 by objective_response
analyses.result_summary: feature_002 mean: responders 3.820 vs non-responders 3.796 (diff +0.024); Welch t = +4.63, p = 3.6e-06.

analyses.code: Welch t-test of each remaining continuous feature by objective_response
analyses.result_summary: All other continuous features (f016, f018, f009, f026, f028, f007, f014, f032, f031, f025, f003, f012, f029) show |t| < 1.6 and p > 0.07 (no detectable marginal association).

analyses.code: value_counts(feature_030)
analyses.result_summary: feature_030 = 0 for all 50000 patients; degenerate, drop from models.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutual adjustment in a multivariable logistic regression, feature_013, feature_008, feature_015, feature_021, feature_001, feature_020, feature_022, feature_024, feature_027, and feature_002 each remain independently associated with objective_response in the directions seen univariately.

feature_005, feature_023, feature_011, feature_017, feature_019, feature_004, feature_006, feature_010, feature_016, feature_018, feature_026, feature_007, feature_014, feature_032, feature_031, feature_025, feature_003, feature_012, feature_028, feature_029, feature_009 are NOT independently associated with objective_response after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.code: statsmodels Logit with all features (continuous standardized); log1p of f022, f024, f018, f009
analyses.result_summary: Significant coefficients (p<1e-7): feature_013 (coef -1.07, OR 0.34, p~0); feature_008 (coef +1.20, OR 3.33, p~0); feature_015 (coef -0.69, OR 0.50, p=2e-108); feature_001 (coef -0.33, OR 0.72, p=1e-82); feature_021 (coef -0.74, OR 0.48, p=4e-64); feature_020 (coef -0.14 std, p=1e-33); log_f022 (-0.10 std, p=2e-12); log_f024 (-0.07 std, p=2e-10); feature_027 (coef -0.43, OR 0.65, p=3e-09); feature_002 (+0.06 std, p=2e-08). All directions match univariate.

analyses.code: Same multivariable model; check non-significant coefs
analyses.result_summary: All listed features have multivariable p > 0.05 (most p > 0.5); no independent association after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015, feature_021, and feature_027 (binary, low-prevalence: ~20%, ~10%, ~3%) act like clinical treatments: their negative effects on objective_response are heterogeneous across patient subgroups (treatment-effect heterogeneity), rather than uniform.

The negative association of feature_013 with response is concentrated entirely in feature_008=1 patients, suggesting feature_013 cancels the favorable effect of feature_008 (effect modification rather than independent prognostic).
The favorable effect of feature_008=1 on objective_response is concentrated entirely in feature_013=0 patients (strong feature_008 x feature_013 interaction).

analyses.code: df.groupby(['feature_013','feature_008'])['objective_response'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Cell response rates -> (013=0,008=0): 0.169 (n=13480); (013=0,008=1): 0.610 (n=9039); (013=1,008=0): 0.150 (n=16444); (013=1,008=1): 0.158 (n=11037). The (013=0,008=1) cell is dramatically higher; all other cells are around 15-17%. Strong synergistic interaction.

analyses.code: Logit with f013 + f008 + f013*f008 main and interaction terms
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = -2.89 (OR 0.056), p ~ 0; main effect of feature_008=1 is OR ~19 in feature_013=0 patients but is wiped out (OR ~1) in feature_013=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_015 on objective_response is concentrated in patients with feature_013=0 and is null in patients with feature_013=1.
The negative effect of feature_015 on objective_response is concentrated in patients with feature_008=1 and is null in patients with feature_008=0.
The negative effects of feature_021 and feature_027 follow the same pattern as feature_015: concentrated in feature_013=0 and feature_008=1 patients.

analyses.code: Stratified 2x2 of feature_015 vs response, within feature_013=0 and feature_013=1
Stratified 2x2 of feature_015 vs response, within feature_008
Stratified 2x2 of feature_021 and feature_027 vs response, within feature_013/feature_008

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015, feature_021, and feature_027 are independent of feature_013 and feature_008 (i.e., treatment prevalence is balanced across the four (f013,f008) cells), so the heterogeneity observed in iter 5 reflects effect modification, not channeling/confounding.

analyses.code: Mean of each treatment within (f013,f008) cells
analyses.result_summary: feature_015 prevalence is 0.198-0.202 across all four cells; feature_021 is 0.097-0.102; feature_027 is 0.029-0.032. Treatments are essentially balanced across the four cells - no meaningful confounding by feature_013 or feature_008.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the joint subgroup defined by feature_013=0 AND feature_008=1, the response rate for patients with NONE of feature_015, feature_021, feature_027 (the 'untreated' subset) is approximately 80%, dramatically higher than the overall cohort baseline of 24%.
In the same subgroup, presence of ANY of feature_015, feature_021, or feature_027 reduces response from ~80% to ~17% (a roughly 60-percentage-point absolute decrease).
Each of feature_015, feature_021, and feature_027 individually produces approximately the same magnitude of response reduction within the (f013=0 AND f008=1) subgroup.

analyses.code: Subset to (f013=0,f008=1); split by any_trt = (f015|f021|f027)
analyses.result_summary: Untreated (n=6325): response 0.798. Treated (n=2714): response 0.171. Risk difference -0.627 (Fisher exact p ~ 0; subgroup OR = 0.052).

analyses.code: In subgroup, isolate each treatment alone (others=0) vs none
analyses.result_summary: vs untreated baseline 0.798: feature_015 only (n=1573) -> 0.182 (diff -0.615); feature_021 only (n=710) -> 0.152 (diff -0.646); feature_027 only (n=189) -> 0.185 (diff -0.613). Two or more (n=242) -> 0.140. The three treatments contribute essentially equivalent and not-clearly-additive harms.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Outside the (feature_013=0 AND feature_008=1) subgroup, the absolute treatment effect of any_trt = (f015|f021|f027) on objective_response is essentially zero (|risk difference| < 0.01) in each of the other three (f013,f008) cells.

In a logistic model with f013, f008, any_trt, and all two-way and the three-way interaction, the three-way interaction coefficient is large and significant, confirming that treatment effect is concentrated in the (f013=0 AND f008=1) subgroup.

analyses.code: Per-cell any_trt effect in each (f013,f008) cell
Logit y ~ f013 + f008 + any_trt + f013:f008 + f013:any_trt + f008:any_trt + f013:f008:any_trt
analyses.result_summary: Three-way interaction coef = +2.89 (p = 1.7e-158). Two-way f008:any_trt = -2.89 (p ~ 0); f013:f008 = -2.89 (p ~ 0). Reading off the cells, treatment OR is ~0.05 in (013=0,008=1) and ~1 elsewhere.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the (f013=0 AND f008=1) subgroup, no other feature in the dataset modifies the any_trt effect to a clinically meaningful or statistically robust degree (i.e., no treatment-effect modifier survives multiple-testing correction).

Within the subgroup, feature_022 (continuous) and feature_001 (3-level) act as PROGNOSTIC markers (independently lowering response) but NOT as TREATMENT effect modifiers (no trt-by-feature interaction).

analyses.code: For each non-defining feature f, fit Logit(y ~ any_trt + f + any_trt*f) within subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Smallest interaction p among 26 candidate modifiers is feature_005 (p=0.04), which does not survive Bonferroni (p_threshold ~0.002); no other p < 0.10 except feature_010 (0.11). No robust treatment-effect modifier within subgroup.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ any_trt + feature_001 + any_trt*feature_001) within subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within subgroup: any_trt main effect coef -3.07 (p=2e-250); feature_001 main effect -0.46 (p=8e-24); interaction +0.07 (p=0.42). feature_001 acts as prognostic, not predictive. Same pattern for feature_022 (interaction p=0.17 within subgroup).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the untreated (f013=0,f008=1,f015=0,f021=0,f027=0) subgroup (n=6325, response 0.80), higher feature_001, higher feature_022, higher feature_020, and higher feature_024 each independently predict lower response, while higher feature_002 predicts marginally higher response.

analyses.code: Multivariable Logit on untreated subgroup with log_f022, f020, f024, f001, f002 (continuous standardized)
analyses.result_summary: feature_001 coef -0.46 (OR 0.63, p=2e-23); feature_020 coef -0.18 std (OR 0.84, p=5e-09); log_f022 coef -0.11 std (p=3e-04); feature_024 coef -0.10 std (p=5e-04); feature_002 coef +0.08 std (OR 1.08, p=0.016). Directions match marginal screen; magnitudes modest within already-favorable subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A shallow decision tree (max_depth=4) fit within the (f013=0 AND f008=1) subgroup splits first on any_trt and predicts class=1 (response) for every untreated leaf and class=0 (no response) for every treated leaf, regardless of other features - confirming any_trt as the dominant within-subgroup driver.

analyses.code: sklearn DecisionTreeClassifier(max_depth=4, min_samples_leaf=200) on subgroup with any_trt + all candidate features
analyses.result_summary: Root split on any_trt <=/> 0.5. Below threshold (untreated): every terminal node predicts class 1. Above threshold (treated): every terminal node predicts class 0. Confirms any_trt is the only first-order driver of the subgroup response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The apparent treatment x feature_022 interaction in the unstratified model is an artifact of feature_022 being correlated with feature_013; once stratified within the (f013=0 AND f008=1) subgroup, the treatment effect does not depend on feature_022.

analyses.code: Pearson correlation feature_022 vs feature_013 across whole cohort, then test trt x log_f022 interaction within subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Mean(feature_022) = 12 in feature_013=0 vs 68 in feature_013=1 (point-biserial r = 0.30). Within (f013=0, f008=1), trt x log_f022 interaction coef = +0.10 (p = 0.17, n.s.). The full-cohort interaction (p=1e-16) is fully explained by f013-f022 correlation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the (f013=0 AND f008=1) subgroup, the response rate decreases monotonically as the number of treatments turned on (count of {f015,f021,f027}=1) increases, with most of the drop occurring on the first treatment.

analyses.code: groupby (f015+f021+f027 count) within subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Count=0 -> 0.798 (n=6325); count=1 (any single) -> ~0.17 (n=2472); count>=2 -> 0.14 (n=242). Most of the drop is going from 0 to 1 treatment; further treatments add little.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Other binary features with no main effect (feature_006, feature_017, feature_019, feature_004, feature_005, feature_023, feature_011) are NOT hidden treatments - they show no heterogeneous treatment effect even when stratified by feature_013 or feature_008 (their effect remains ~0 in every cell).

analyses.code: Stratified 2x2 of each candidate binary vs response within (f013, f008) cells
analyses.result_summary: For each of f006, f017, f019, f004, f005, f023, f011: |risk difference| in every (f013,f008) cell is < 0.015 and stratum-specific p > 0.10. None show the heterogeneity pattern of f015/f021/f027.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP: For the binary treatment any_trt = (feature_015=1 OR feature_021=1 OR feature_027=1), the treatment reduces objective_response by ~62 percentage points (from ~80% to ~17%, OR ~0.05) ONLY in the joint subgroup defined by feature_013=0 AND feature_008=1 (n=9039); in all other patients (the remaining 41,000 with feature_013=1 or feature_008=0) the treatment effect is null (|risk diff| < 0.01). The unfavorable values feature_013=1 and feature_008=0 each independently suppress this treatment effect; both must be 'favorable' (f013=0 AND f008=1) for the treatment to act.

FINAL PROGNOSTIC PROFILE (untreated patients): The untreated 'best-prognosis' subgroup defined by feature_013=0 AND feature_008=1 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_027=0 has response rate ~80%; within this group, response is further raised by feature_001=0 (vs 1 or 2), lower feature_022, lower feature_020, lower feature_024, and (modestly) higher feature_002, but no additional binary feature substantially modifies it.

analyses.code: df['resp_subgroup'] = (f013==0)&(f008==1); df['any_trt']=(f015|f021|f027); groupby([resp_subgroup,any_trt]).response.mean()
analyses.result_summary: Joint subgroup response table: outside subgroup, untreated -> 0.161 (n=28519); outside subgroup, treated -> 0.154 (n=12442) [diff -0.006]; inside subgroup, untreated -> 0.798 (n=6325); inside subgroup, treated -> 0.171 (n=2714) [diff -0.627, OR 0.052, Fisher p ~ 0]. Treatment effect is concentrated in 18% of cohort.

analyses.code: Multivariable Logit on untreated subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within untreated subgroup (n=6325): feature_001 OR=0.63 per level (p=2e-23); feature_020 OR=0.84 per std (p=5e-09); feature_022 OR=0.89 per log-std (p=3e-04); feature_024 OR=0.91 per std (p=5e-04); feature_002 OR=1.08 per std (p=0.016).
